activities.name: January calibration run
activities.description: Thought I would be much slower but just managed to squeeze another month of running where the first digit in the pace is “8”. My humble exercise goal is more such months.

activities.name: Dayton loop under steady rain with Tamara
activities.description: Her positivity made us forget the rain. Thank you, friend. No stops and just a few 15 second walks.

activities.name: Was it a run or a swim?
activities.description: Bridle Trails was muddier than I’ve ever seen it. Very fun run with the gang, and a fond farewell to [strava://athletes/111080884/15]Vladislav Zotea. Good luck in Chicago… we will miss you!

activities.name: Bean run
activities.description: I need to work out how / where to try to emulate this amazing achievement by a dog smaller than Bean. See comments

activities.name: Not so great
activities.description: Had been hoping  for further, but so be it. Many walk breaks

activities.name: Slow hills with Lauren and Tamara
Bean run/walk trying out my new highly breathable Territory Run cap
Short hill repeats on a sunny afternoon
QA crown + 3 surprises
activities.description: Most of this morning’s run with Expect Delays

activities.name: Woof
activities.description: Bean sets a PR… at least for moving time.

activities.name: Off leash on Cannon Beach
activities.description: Roadtrip day #1. Love Cannon Beach in winter!

activities.name: To Ecola Point for sunrise
activities.description: How can this coastline be so beautiful?

activities.name: Out to the lighthouse, then down to the beach
activities.description: Yaquina head is pretty spectacular on a beautiful morning

activities.name: Bean wears the watch
activities.description: We walk 1.5 miles; she runs 4.3

activities.name: Seeking sun on a frosty morning with Lauren
Road trip visits a city called Seattle
activities.description: Very pretty. Not as pretty as the Oregon coast though!

activities.name: Good friends; bad run
activities.description: More and more walking required. Thank you for staying with me Martha, Tam, and Anita

activities.name: Along the beach at Penn Cove
Penn Cove is a mirror
activities.description: Have we ever had such a string of clear days in January?

activities.name: Full loop of Ebey’s beach and bluff
activities.description: With Chris who very bravely handled the exposure on the bluff

activities.name: Penn Cove near sunset
activities.description: Thinking a little about mortality after buying the book shown.

activities.name: To Retreat to meet my mates
Snow happens
activities.description: Big thick flakes falling

activities.name: Careful on the icy streets
activities.description: And lots of stops to catch my breath.

activities.name: Actual pace these days
activities.description: Lots of walks; no stops

activities.name: Can still do a short pace ladder on the local hill
activities.description: Used to be able to do 10 laps; but I’ll take 5 today.

activities.name: Most of the Expect Delays run
activities.description: Frosty but sunny!

activities.name: Slow slumped slice
activities.description: But good to get 6 miles in. + new shoes!

activities.name: Bean “run”
activities.description: By which we mean a little jogging between sniff spots

activities.name: Thigh deep powder
activities.description: At least for the first 100 yards before we took advantage of some trails.

activities.name: Good ski on the Sun Valley Nordic trails
activities.description: With Chris before lunch and then a lot more solo after lunch

activities.name: Long haul  on the Harriman trail
activities.description: First few miles with Chris and Patty (and Freddy!), then many more by myself. Bit of a headwind on the return.

activities.name: Prairie Creek out and back
activities.description: With Chris and Sharon and Ed. Slightly overcast day, and warmer than the other days we have had here.

activities.name: The Umatilla running high and muddy
activities.description: We spent the night in Pendleton en route back to Seattle from Sun Valley

activities.name: Baby’s first mile walk
activities.description: I had heart surgery 10 days ago, and have been home from the hospital for 4 days. Getting a little stronger every day. It’ll be a few months till I’m back running I think, so walking with friends is what I do. Thank you [strava://athletes/8506527/14]Herve Jamrozik for keeping up with my cracking 2mph pace!

activities.name: Walk with Lauren beside a still lake
activities.description: Thank you friend!

activities.name: Pulled up the hill by friends
activities.description: Thank you Mintu, Anita, Herve, Ana, and Chris

activities.name: Walk with Monica and Chris while runners ran
activities.description: Felt good. Still getting noticably stronger each day.

activities.name: Gloriously warm afternoon in the Arboretum
Seward Park with Chris, Martha, and Christine. And Bean!
Expedition to Petit Pierre
activities.description:
With Herve, Ana, Mintu, and Anita. Highly recommended! 

https://maps.app.goo.gl/MWngqGwYeCBeSNqw7?g_st=com.google.maps.preview.copy

activities.name: Stairs of West Queen Anne
activities.description: With Chris, Martha, Christine and Carol

activities.name: Baby’s first jog
activities.description: I am 4 weeks on from heart surgery, and I got permission from my wonderful cardiologist [strava://athletes/35557616/14]Tom Richardson to jog a little. Felt wonderful even if I could only go 100 yards at a time before walking. All in good time.

activities.name: Hawthorn Hills walk with Chris and Bean
activities.description: While the runners run.

activities.name: 2nd jog after heart surgery
activities.description: It’s easy to improve when you start at a very low baseline! First jog/walk was at 13 min/mile. This one was a little faster. Also got my heart rate up to a reasonable level.

activities.name: Each little jog is a little better than the last
activities.description: That’s a good feeling!

activities.name: Glorious Retiro Park
activities.description: Very peaceful in the morning.

activities.name: Hills around Guadalupe
activities.description: We toured the famous monastery and saw the icon of the virgin in the morning, then set off for this ramble in the hills, getting back to our paradores just before the rain hit.

activities.name: Oak trees and water hazards
activities.description: After a visit to the Roman theater in Mérida

activities.name: Monfragüe National Park
activities.description: Morning on a sheep drovers trail through classic Extremadura oak trees, meeting a horse rider and very protective sheep dogs, then up a ridge into the park where we saw some cork-stripped oak trunks and down to a reservoir on the Tejas. Longest walk since my surgery. Tired afterwards, but no more so than everyone else!

activities.name: Pyrenean oak forest, and La Routa Del Emperador
activities.description: We walked from Charles V’s final home up through the Oak forests, and after lunch along the cobblestone route he was carried to get there

activities.name: Into Castile
activities.description:
A via pecuaria (livestock route) in the morning in northern Extremadura, then the old Roman road that became the Routa de la Plata into the kingdom of Castille.

(The straight line in the middle is a bus ride)

activities.name: La Peña de Francia down to La Alberca
La Alberca to San Martin del Castañar for Chris’ birthday
activities.description: Muddy at first, then a high granite plateau then lunch and finally into the town with its medieval bull ring.

activities.name: Around and above the Guggenheim
activities.description: Got to Bilbao this morning…. Raining solidly most of the day unfortunately. Half walk half jog

activities.name: Another short jog/walk
activities.description: No improvement to speak of. Hmmm.

activities.name: Little dog jog with the little dog
activities.description: Plus photos of other dogs we met in Spain: Big, crazy big, and Jeff Koons big

activities.name: Post surgery baseline for my calibration run
activities.description: Longest run/walk to date. Felt good to push it a little. I have a long way to go to come back, but I am optimistic.

activities.name: Doggy joggy
activities.description: Bean was raring to go, but my legs were feeling it from yesterday’s massive (3.7!) mileage :-)

activities.name: First Dayton jog with the new plumbing
activities.description: Another distance notch… and didn’t backslide on pace. It’s easy to improve when you start from the bottom!

activities.name: 40s of improvement on my calibration run
activities.description: Felt tough but good

activities.name: Not feeling it
activities.description: But not beating myself up

activities.name: Best run so far
activities.description: Fewer walks -> better pace. And finished off with the classic Bean tongue-bath. Apologies to non-dog people!

activities.name: Walk with Hans and Svea
activities.description: While the runners ran.

activities.name: Slight improvement every day
activities.description: Makes David a happy boy.

activities.name: Talking books with Martha
activities.description: And jogging a little. Very fun.

activities.name: Monotonic improvement on the calibration run
activities.description: Very tough though. Highest average heart rate in a long time.

activities.name: Cardiac Rehab Treadmill
activities.description: (4 min Jog, 3 min walk) * 4 at 2% grade, and peaking at 10 min/mile

activities.name: Doing what my rehab therapist tells me to do
activities.description: “Slow down so you don’t have to walk as often, and try to go a bit longer”. Made it 2 slow miles before I had to start taking walk breaks.

activities.name: Walk with Expect Delays
activities.description: Did about a mile of jogging along the way

activities.name: So-so
activities.description: Not as good as last week, but you can’t improve on every run

activities.name: Cardiac Rehab Treadmill
activities.description: (Walk 2min, jog 5min) x4 at 2% and 10-ish min/mile. 275’ of climb.

activities.name: River walk with Liz and Chris and Bean
Treadmill at Cardiac Rehab
activities.description: Twice a week for the next 7 or so weeks I will be doing 30 mins of treadmill followed by a little strength and stretching. It’s a good program!

activities.name: Green Lake Countdown is full!
activities.description: 50th person signed up this morning. Looking forward to an exciting day tomorrow.

activities.name: Cardiac rehab Treadmill
activities.description: Easier session at 0 grade for most of it. 5 min run at 5.7 to 6.2 mph, then 2 min walk repeated 3 times. Photo from yesterday left to right of the winner, #2, and one of the equal #3s.

activities.name: Phinney Steeps
activities.description: Toughest run since surgery. Had to take a stop at the top of several hills, even though I walked them!  In theory I am improving my conditioning.

activities.name: Scouting new trails for Expect Delays
activities.description:
Soft tread; gentle rollers on the west side, a few gullies in the east. Great visibility. Small, but worthy!

Bean likes them too.

activities.name: Tangletown calibration
activities.description: Fractionally faster and  a bit easier, so that’s progress. Heart rate *much* lower, but that’s probably due to not having coffee before I went out.

activities.name: Treadmill at cardiac rehab
activities.description: 2% grade, 5.7 - 6.2 mph. 5 min run, 2 min walk

activities.name: Back to Bridle Trails with Expect Delays
activities.description: I ran by myself… Pleased to not hold the others up

activities.name: Cool in the canyon
activities.description: After dropping the car for service. Nice coffee with Herve afterwards.

activities.name: New distance record since surgery
activities.description: Many walk breaks but decent overall pace. Perhaps I *am* getting stronger as my friends keep telling me.

activities.name: Cardiac Rehab Treadmill
activities.description: I do seem to be improving!

activities.name: Better again
activities.description: Seeing the number 9 as the first digit of the pace is very heartening.

activities.name: Around Llandover with Expect Delays
activities.description: Fun and beautiful Father’s Day run and hike

activities.name: Rehab treadmill
activities.description: 5 min intervals at 9:50 pace and increasing grade: 1,2,2.5,3%

activities.name: Dayton loop… tough
activities.description: Harder than I was expecting… many mini walks

activities.name: Orange theory strength
activities.description: Very Un-strong!

activities.name: 5000m on the erg
activities.description: First time in 20 years. Good for the core I imagine

activities.name: Decent 4-miler
activities.description: Was able to go 2 miles before taking a walk break. Also paused at the top of 50th.

activities.name: Out and back while friends loop the lake
activities.description: 2 smooth miles then lots of stops. Got hot in the sunshine on the way back.

activities.name: Calibration Tangle
activities.description: Didn’t feel faster than last time, but was. Getting slowly stronger.

activities.name: Down to the wetlands
activities.description: Meeting Chris and Julie down there

activities.name: Lots of stops
activities.description: after about a mile and a half. Nice to run into Martha.

activities.name: Two figure 8’s of the canyon
activities.description: Tried to go easy on the first so I could have the satisfaction of a negative split, but it wasn’t to be. Heart rate data seemed to be bogus, at least in lap 1.

activities.name: Cardiac Rehab Treadmill
activities.description: 5 min runs, 2 min walks, ramping up to 8:30 pace by the end. Good stuff.

activities.name: To the canyon and then figure 8
activities.description: Better than a few days ago… I do like that figure 8 loop

activities.name: Little 1/2 miler
activities.description: That got my heart rate up!

activities.name: Run out; walk back
activities.description: Kept up with expect delays on the outbound, walked back with Monica. Thank you!

activities.name: Cardiac rehab erg
activities.description: 5000m in 25:10. Max heart rate around 140

activities.name: Doggy Disco
activities.description: She dragged behind me… too many good smells.

activities.name: Not feeling it this morning
activities.description: Stiff and weak. Not sure why.

activities.name: Rehab treadmill
activities.description: 2 miles at 10 min pace, then 2 min walk then a 4 min ramp up 3% grade. Felt pretty good.

activities.name: Expect Delays on July 4th
activities.description: Beautiful morning; great group

activities.name: Doggy intervals
activities.description: Fast running paused at random every 30-90 seconds when Bean decides she REALLY needs to sniff something

activities.name: Expect Delays in the Arb.
activities.description: Small group, but we had a good time

activities.name: Cardiac rehab treadmill
activities.description: 10 min/mile pace; ramping up to 4%. 2 min walk breaks every 1/2 mile

activities.name: Seeking shade with a snake by the lake
Ran into Mintu along the way
5000m  on the erg at Cardiac rehab
activities.description: Averaged 105 watts

activities.name: Not exactly running with the gang
activities.description: But meeting them before and after with Miss Bean.

activities.name: Treadmill at cardiac rehab
activities.description: 4% grade . 4 mins at 10 min/mile, then 3 min walk. All x4

activities.name: Warm even in the shade
activities.description: Pretty good for this delicate flower. Two stops and a couple of walks

activities.name: Cardiac Rehab Treadmill
activities.description: 4 4-min intervals with 2-3 mins walking between. Intervals at 10 min/mile pace at 4-5% grade

activities.name: El Palo Alto
activities.description: With Grace… and revisited the place where I lived in 1988-9!

activities.name: To Hannibal’s Pass with John Hoyte and family and friends
activities.description: John lead the team who took an elephant over the pass in 1959. Such a memorable day!

activities.name: More of Hannibal’s likely trail
activities.description: Yesterday I visited the only pass where 30,000 troops + horses and elephants could plausibly camp together, which is clearly stated by Polybius, the best source. Today I took in the obvious connection from Le Plenay to this pass route. This trail became a major Roman route through the alps within 70 years of Hannibal’s time according to the excellent signage.

activities.name: Riverbed scramble towards “White Rock”
activities.description:
Polybius talks about a prominent white rock that provided some shelter for his troops while they were being harassed by locals. Bramans (en route to the Col de Clapier) has an extremely prominent white rock face. Whether this is what Polybius was talking about we cannot say. Didn’t get near enough to really assess how his troops could have benefitted from it, since I’m not quite as tough as Hannibal —I didn’t want to get wet feet.

(Trail actually shows the return journey)

activities.name: Final hike in the Val Cenis
activities.description: Not part of Hannibal’s route… just beautiful.

activities.name: The corn fields of Lyon airport
activities.description: Gotta love that farm land survives where the long term parking ends. On my way home now.

activities.name: Little hill repeats after a fun social time
activities.description: As I was about to take off  I ran into a friend I hadn’t seen for a couple of years, then struck up a conversation with another person who I think is going to become a friend. Gotta love Green Lake!

activities.name: Open comb, heading to coffee at Pacha
activities.description: Video memory of a beautiful valley in the French Alps from Thursday.

activities.name: Cardiac Rehab treadmill
activities.description: 6.2 mph at 3-5% for 4 mins at a time, x4

activities.name: It’s getting better
activities.description: 90 seconds improvement on my standard calibration run. Still a long way to go, but progress is progress.

activities.name: Tried to go slow, but still overheated
activities.description: In the furnace of 62F

activities.name: Cardiac rehab treadmill
activities.description: 4 minute intervals at 8:25 pace… at 0,1,2% grade with 3 min walks in between

activities.name: Jog then walk with Chris and Bean
Tinkham Peak adventure with Steve and Brian
Cardiac Rehab treadmill
activities.description:
Almost my last time with the great crew at UW cardiac rehab. I will miss them!

4x 4:30 minute 8:30 pace intervals with 2:30 walk between them. 0% grade today as I was feeling a little head cold-ish

activities.name: Track workout as planned by Steve V
activities.description: Couldn’t quite do what he planned, but I tried!

activities.name: Fun until it got hot
activities.description: But the afterparty was excellent. Thank you Anita and Mintu!

activities.name: Final cardiac rehab
activities.description: 4x 4 minute intervals at 8:30 pace, at 1 and then 2% with 3 minute walk in between. Thank you Steve V!

activities.name: Snake = Lake
activities.description: Same distance but almost full shade

activities.name: Easy Ravenna Canyon
activities.description: With Mintu and Anita. Not hot, but definitely humid!

activities.name: Many stops
activities.description: And sluggish

activities.name: The flat parts of the Expect Delays run
activities.description: Good to catch up with all these fine people most weekends.

activities.name: Figure 8 of the lake
activities.description: Smooth for 3 miles, then got too hot… stopped a few times. Pretty good overall.

activities.name: Leaving the lid up?
activities.description: Tried to do my “calibration run” but went out a bit fast, so cut it short. Felt pretty good though.

activities.name: Cooler -> Further
activities.description: This summer I’ve been pooped after 2-3 miles but I made it to 4 this morning with temps in the 50s. Nice.

activities.name: Track time with Jason!
activities.description: Feeling a bit hung over after 1 beer last night. Wow

activities.name: Roche Harbor on a silent Saturday morning
activities.description:
Just beautiful.
One photo of Chris and Anne from yesterday night

activities.name: Young Hill and beyond with Geof, Anne, and Chris
San Juan Island erg
activities.description: Chris did it first, then I got on for 5000m. Good sweaty fun.

activities.name: Delicate flower wilted at 62F
activities.description: Several stops in 2nd half

activities.name: Second lunch on Second Burroughs with Roger
activities.description: Misty, windy and a few raindrops. A great day on Mount Rainier

activities.name: Pretty good comb-over
activities.description: Was expecting I’d need to walk more than I did.

activities.name: Calibration run: 6 months after heart surgery
activities.description: Every month it gets better. See attached pace comparison. Didn’t know it would take this long, but getting used to the idea that it’s a very slow process.

activities.name: Hilly Carkeek and Blue Ridge
activities.description: Fun with Expect Delays

activities.name: 2x800 + 6x400
activities.description: Supposed to be doing 4x800. Hope to work up to that.

activities.name: Supposedly easy
activities.description: First two miles felt easy… but overheated as usual in mile 3. But still longest run without a walk.

activities.name: Dayton loop on a warm morning
activities.description: Stopped 3 times to cool down, but was still a soggy yeti at the end. Looks like it will be another week before the mornings cool down around here.

activities.name: Short run; good run
activities.description: Good distance for me when it’s in the 60s.

activities.name: There’s nothing wrong with getting a little lost
activities.description: Deliciously cool and rainy Sydney morning.

activities.name: Treadmill: 1km intervals
activities.description: Starting from 10:30 pace. Finishing at 8:30 pace

activities.name: To the Gibberagong Track and Back
activities.description: Getting slower and slower, but it’s a beautiful morning, so whatever.

activities.name: Lane Cove National Park
activities.description: And a visit with the Turra-Champions afterwards

activities.name: Coups Creek with Mark
activities.description: Beautiful!

activities.name: The Gibbaragong track: split personality
activities.description: Wide and flat at the top, then a precipitous drop down to the creek. Doubtless much more fun on the remainder of the trail to Bobbin Head but that would have meant a knee deep creek crossing which I was not up for this morning.

activities.name: Back home to the shady snake
activities.description: 2 stops in the last mile.

activities.name: Quick one before an airport run
Truncated calibration run
activities.description: Got gaslit by my watch which told me I was way behind the pace… but actually I was only a little behind. Yes — I need to pay less attention to that little bugger.

activities.name: September Calibration run
activities.description: Happy to see I’m still improving a little.

activities.name: It’s getting 🆒 in the mornings
activities.description: Hooray for 53F! First 3 miles felt good then I stopped a few times in the final mile. Not bad.

activities.name: Track work is better with friends
activities.description: Mintu accompanied me on 4x800, and Martha, Tamara, and Wendy were all there too. No photos unfortunately.

activities.name: Better again. It’s been a great week so far.
activities.description: At this rate I’m going to have to stop whining about my slow recovery pretty soon!

activities.name: Easy with 30 second pickups
activities.description: [strava://athletes/385803/11]Mino Giunta prescribed these for me about 6 months ago, and [strava://athletes/35557616/14]Tom Richardson also recommends them. Eventually I get the message. Every half mile I did 30 seconds at full speed followed by a minute of walking.

activities.name: Short and sweet with Expect Delays
To the Potomac
activities.description: Via a forest trail; back on streets

activities.name: Only slightly off course in Rock Creek Park
Bride Runs before her wedding
activities.description: We are excited!

activities.name: Best run since heart surgery
activities.description: 30s/mile faster than last time, mostly because of the deliciously cool morning. Whole run was at or under 50F. That’s the way, uh huh uh huh, I like it.

activities.name: Track work with Martha
activities.description: 4x800 at increasing pace. Final 800 took 4:08. It’s so much easier to do this with a friend than by yourself. Thank you Martha!

activities.name: Hartstene Point on Harstine Island
activities.description: Yes, it’s confusing.

activities.name: Another Hartstene looper
activities.description: Photos from yesterday on the spit at the end of the peninsula.

activities.name: Last Harstine morning
activities.description: Gentle hills and no traffic

activities.name: 50s good; 40s better!
activities.description: Longest run in 8 months in deliciously cool weather

activities.name: Track work with Martha again
activities.description: Wasn’t feeling up to 4x800… did 1 of them and then some 400s. Good enough!

activities.name: Flat four with a couple of stops
activities.description: Nice last 3/4 of a mile though.

activities.name: 12 minute VO2max test
activities.description: The so called “Cooper test”. It suggests a score of 38.7 ml/kg/min, which is pretty good for my age. Garmin suggests 45, but I don’t trust it.

activities.name: Windy 6 with Expect Delays
activities.description: Really fun with the gang

activities.name: October Calibration run
activities.description: Surprisingly, the almost linear improvement has continued in the 9th such run since surgery 7+ months ago. Heartening!

activities.name: Queen Anne Loop with Roger
activities.description: Beautiful afternoon ending with a mandatory Macrina visit

activities.name: Trying to keep up with the women… they went easy on me
activities.description: Route looks a bit like an elephant

activities.name: Low fiber breakfast at Blue Star
activities.description: There was no running home after this!

activities.name: Soggy joggy Sunday
activities.description: But company and coffee at the VP cafe made it all better.

activities.name: Lake and ridge figure 8
activities.description: 2nd time on this long (for me) route.

activities.name: Book talk and baked goods with Dave R
activities.description: Great to catch up, and gotta love Coyles on a week day!

activities.name: Vashon in October
activities.description: Primo fall!

activities.name: Sluggish recently
activities.description: But good to get out there even for a short one.

activities.name: The Beautiful SVT with Expect Delays
activities.description: Longest run in a year! Felt good.

activities.name: Doggy joggy and ballot droppy
activities.description: So many squirrels to strain towards

activities.name: November Calibration Tangle
activities.description: Linear improvement seems to continue! I took another 50s off, which is surprising. And heartening.

activities.name: Little leg stretcher in the rain
Pushing it under heavy rain
activities.description: Tough one.

activities.name: Expect Delays: Peak Fall in the City
activities.description: Glorious around the lake and neighborhood.

activities.name: Little bit of Little Si with Little Bean and Chris
activities.description: And a lot of Apple Crostada afterwards

activities.name: To the gym for a little strength and sauna
activities.description: Another pretty morning at Green Lake. 30s pickups along the way.

activities.name: Flat runs are never easy
activities.description: Not sure why but I always do better on rolling hills than flats. This was tough.

activities.name: Track time for David
activities.description: Didn’t have any buddies along this time so  some 800s became 400s and 200s. So be it.

activities.name: A billion leaves in Bridle Trails
activities.description: But no mud, so I got off easy.

activities.name: Treadmill intervals
activities.description:
4 minute intervals, like Steve taught me.
Pace increasing from 10:30 to 8:30
The danger of a planned 3 mile run that comes close to your house after 1.

activities.name: Dramatically better
activities.description: Than recent runs… strangely the only difference was that I did 3 mins of floppy fascia exercises beforehand… it must have lengthened my stride a little. Intriguing.

activities.name: 5 good Kenwood Hills
activities.description: Slight mid to laps 4 and 5: instead of taking a break at the bottom, I took one half way up.

activities.name: Billy Bonks in Fremont
activities.description: This is perhaps what happens when you wake at 5, and have 1/2 a bowl of granola then go back to sleep for 2 hours, then eat nothing more

activities.name: Billy Bonker jogs home
activities.description: Cut my 5 mile short in Fremont and walked up the hill before this little effort

activities.name: Easy trail miles with Bean testing new shoes
activities.description: 3 with Miss B; 2 without. All deliciously flat!

activities.name: Expect Sogginess
activities.description: Great crew makes up for lousy weather

activities.name: Short but spicy at the end
activities.description: Was able to run the last mile at a little below 8:30, which felt good

activities.name: Visiting Liz in HMB
activities.description: Coastal Trail and Half Moon Bay Golf Cart trails. Pretty glorious

activities.name: Checking out the golfers at the Ritz Carlton
Cows and Clifftops at Cowell Ranch
activities.description: Short jog with Bean, who was a bit scared of the cows.

activities.name: Sunshine on the Burke Gilman with Expect Delays
Tasty slice of toast
activities.description: Still improving on this course too.

activities.name: Helping my friend Mintu train for a BQ
activities.description: He did 13 downhill; I did 4 up; 4 down. All very successful.

activities.name: Let’s call it “1 minute intervals”
activities.description: Not necessarily what I was planning but I’ll take it.

activities.name: Reindeer Romp 5 at Redmond Watershed
activities.description: With Mintu, Anita, Rich, Daniel, and Rimma! Lots of walk breaks for me.

activities.name: There’s always a new route in Tangletown
activities.description: This one was very gentle on the hills. One 30s stop

activities.name: Easy pace; tough effort
activities.description: For me at least. Nice to run with Tamara and Lauren

activities.name: Break in the rain with Bean
activities.description: Photo from when she was on beach, off leash over Thanksgiving

activities.name: Psychological boost on foggy trails
activities.description: I was a bit disappointed by my performance in a trail race last weekend and thought I could do better. I mapped out an arboretum trail course of about the same distance and the same elevation and was able to do it about 40s/mile faster. Hooray.

activities.name: Birthday leg stretcher
activities.description: With the 2nd half mile near the 8 min pace

activities.name: Big group today!
activities.description: Great fun running with this crew. We keep each other young (ish!) And a photo of Chris at the Green Lake luminaries last night

activities.name: Mini Comb
activities.description: Hoped to do more, but wasn’t feeling it.

activities.name: Two lakes for two mates
activities.description: Thank you Mintu!

activities.name: December Calibration Tangle
activities.description:
Tough effort on a cold and blustery day. Good conditions!
The linear pace improvement on this course I had been experiencing for eight months had to come to an end sometime , and it seems that time is now. Tiny 15 second improvement today, but still an improvement!

activities.name: Sniff City with Bean
activities.description: Run for 1 minute,  sniff for 20 seconds. Repeat.

activities.name: Yarrow Point with Expect Delays
activities.description: Shortest day of the year; dark and a bit drizzly. Lots of sunny company though!

activities.name: Partied a little too much last night
activities.description: But boy it was fun

activities.name: Back to Dayton
activities.description: Love those temps in the 30s!

activities.name: Doing what I should do, but never do
activities.description: Staying in Zone 2.

activities.name: Christmas sog dog jog
activities.description: The only thing Bean hates more than walking in the rain is running in it

activities.name: Humbled by the hills in Mosman
activities.description: But was with Grace and ran into Pip and Andrew Tyndale so there were rewards to the punishment

activities.name: To meet the Middle Head walking group
activities.description: Downhills in the shade works well for me! Pub photo from yesterday

activities.name: “A kookaburra took your muffin!”
activities.description: Overheard and witnessed at Frenchies cafe after this great headland walk with Pip, Andrew and our crowd
